speaker: charlie
text: Now here's the science story that's got me absolutely buzzing today. California firefighters are testing a device developed by former NASA engineers that uses sound waves to put out flames, and it works in milliseconds.

speaker: alice
text: This isn't science fiction anymore Alex, it's happening right now in 2026! The technology works by using specific sound frequencies to disrupt the chemical reactions that sustain fire, essentially cutting off the flame at a molecular level.

speaker: charlie
text: Imagine the possibilities for bushfire fighting here in Australia. No more water bombers, no more foam, just pure sound waves stopping fires dead in their tracks. The NASA connection makes perfect sense too - space technology coming down to solve earthbound problems.
